voters.name: Rose
voters.comment: I mean no offense by this. But this might be the most painful song ever posted to the League. It is however better than the original. Doot for that.

voters.name: Mike
voters.comment: I think I just don't like the Cramps, I could not hardly stand this.

voters.name: Sarah
voters.comment: I can appreciate what this was going for (after listening to the original) but man it is just not for me

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: The original does not openly break down like that. It's more country so his voice just cracks. Lux Interior interpreted this as a breakdown and pushed it.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Never heard them before but I admire a band that gets a lil freaky with it.

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: I don't even know them from Wednesday, I had to go look up it up to see what you meant - ahhhh the iconic dance scene! I never really listened to the Cramps but I liked this

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I guess this means all of our periods have synchronized because I just gave everybody The Cramps.

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: Two cramps! I think I liked this one better than the other Cramps song. I keep going back and forth on if I actually like this or not but it definitely gets stuck in my head

voters.name: Mike
voters.comment: I said it on the other one, I just don't like the Cramps I think. I could see how the garbledness could be fun for some people, but it doesn't work for me.

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: "Called to the Devil and the Devil said "Hey." Love. Another fun band name that I've never heard of though I do recognize this song.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I'm not entirely sure when it's supposed to be the devil talking or the singer but best I can tell this is a song about needing someone to talk to you so badly that you reach out to the devil who agrees to help you sort your shit out. The devil lets the singer know that it's fine to take time to yourself to take of yourself and not worry about others. Then, the singer throws it back on the devil when the devil complains about being overworked and too tired. The devil responds by saying that the singer wants everyone to need them or they would let it got or at least accept help. Finally, the singer resolves to just let everything go.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: I DO love RKS. Some of their songs are hit or miss for me, but this one was a hit for sure. The tone of "..dang" was so good.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I am so obsessed with this song that it scares me sometimes. If no one else likes this, it will only confirm that a bug crawled in my brain and has taken me over just to listen to this song repeatedly.
